# Hearth Handbook

> A digital welcome mat for Wisconsin towns — hand-curated local restaurants, shops, services, parks, and coupons for every community we cover.

Hearth Handbook helps new residents and visitors discover the best of their Wisconsin town. Each guide is curated by a real person who lives nearby — no scraped listings, no algorithmic feeds. Sponsors are local businesses; coupons are exclusive to the guide.

## Pages

- [Home](/): Pick your Wisconsin town by location, ZIP, or browse.
- [Towns directory](/towns): Browse every Wisconsin town in the Hearth Handbook directory.
- [About](/about): Mission, coverage, and the team behind Hearth Handbook.
- [Sign in](/login): Realtor and resident sign-in.
- [Privacy](/privacy): How we handle your data.
- [Terms](/terms): Terms of service.

## Town guides

- [Grafton](/grafton): Riverside village built around Bridge Street's brick storefronts.
- [Cedarburg](/cedarburg): Historic downtown, covered bridge, year-round festivals.
- [Mequon](/mequon): Leafy lakeshore neighborhoods and farm-to-table favorites.
- [Port Washington](/port-washington): Lake Michigan harbor town with the iconic 1860 lighthouse.
- [Thiensville](/thiensville): Compact, walkable village along the Milwaukee River.
- [Saukville](/saukville): Riverside village on the Milwaukee River.
- [Fredonia](/fredonia): Quiet northern Ozaukee community surrounded by farms and forests.
- [Belgium](/belgium): Lake Michigan village with deep heritage and wide-open countryside.
